bits.premise: Texas winter was cold enough that a low-battery smoke detector became her improvised sleep music.
Calling her crackhead father an entrepreneur leads into a salary joke about drug addiction.

lines.text: What's up, Austin?
lines.label: fluff

lines.text: I've been here for about a year now.
lines.label: setup

lines.text: And there's one thing I knew about Texas
lines.label: setup

lines.text: before I live in here.
lines.label: setup

lines.text: It's hot, right?
lines.label: setup

lines.text: But it gets pretty cold at night.
lines.label: setup

lines.text: It got pretty cold during the winter.
lines.label: setup

lines.text: So cold that I needed help to sleep at night.
lines.label: setup

lines.text: So I started listening to Negro Spirituals.
lines.label: punchline

lines.text: Yeah, you're like, this crazy bitch has got like
lines.label: setup

lines.text: Wade in the water radio on Spotify.
lines.label: setup

lines.text: That's fucking crazy.
lines.label: setup

lines.text: No, it was just me being too lazy to change the battery
lines.label: setup

lines.text: in my smoke detector.
lines.label: punchline

lines.text: Hell yeah.
lines.label: fluff

lines.text: Uh...
lines.label: fluff

lines.text: Uh, I like to think that my dad was somebody to look up to
lines.label: setup

lines.text: as, like, an entrepreneur.
lines.label: setup

lines.text: It was just a really nice way of saying
lines.label: setup

lines.text: that he was a full-time crackhead.
lines.label: punchline

lines.text: I don't know if you know this, but, uh, Zip Recruiter...
lines.label: setup

lines.text: [squeals]
lines.label: fluff

lines.text: Uh, uh, uh...
lines.label: fluff

lines.text: Excuse-- fuck.
lines.label: fluff

lines.text: A sponsor of Kill Tony actually used to report
lines.label: setup

lines.text: the average salary.
lines.label: punchline
